economic_threshold.en: DOA guideline: 2 BPH per hill at booting stage or 5 per hill at heading stage, adjusted for natural enemy (spider) presence.

notes.en: Higher moisture levels and indiscriminate insecticide use during the vegetative stage known to trigger BPH outbreaks; most damaging during late vegetative and reproductive stages.

description.en: Heavy infestations produce hopper burn — leaves dry and turn brown after feeding, with patches of burned plants often lodged. Also transmits grassy stunt and ragged stunt viruses, compounding losses.

description.quote: Heavy infestations produce symptoms of hopper burn. Leaves dry and turn brown after insect feeding, and patches of burned plants are often lodged. Acts as a disease vector for grassy stunt and ragged stunt virus diseases.

economic_threshold.quote: booting stage: 2 per hill; heading stage: 5 per hill threshold

favored_conditions.quote: Indiscriminate use of insecticides during vegetative stage known to cause BPH outbreaks. Higher moisture levels support multiplication
